auth_notes: Most proprietary NLP services utilize a standard Header-based authentication (e.g., 'Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>' or 'X-API-KEY'). Some enterprise versions may require OAuth2 Client Credentials flow.
rate_limits: Typically 10-50 requests per second depending on the subscription tier; usage-based quotas often apply to total characters or tokens processed.
gotchas: Payload size limits: Large documents may need to be chunked before sending to the HTTP Request node.
Timeouts: Complex NLP tasks (like summarization) can take longer than the default 30s n8n timeout.
Data structure: Responses are often deeply nested, requiring complex JSON path expressions or a Code node to parse.
client_must_provide: Model ID or specific deployment name
research_notes: Integration will be handled via the 'HTTP Request' node. The developer will need to set the Method to POST, provide the URL, and manually construct the JSON body. Since no native node exists, the 'Simplify Response' toggle won't be available, so a 'Set' node or 'Code' node will likely be required to clean up the output for subsequent workflow steps.

Since no native node exists, the 'Simplify Response' toggle won't be available, so a 'Set' node or 'Code' node will likely be required to clean up the output for subsequent workflow steps.
